tests.prompt: Run /journal for 2026-04-03 (evening). Today's captures include: 'NVDA broke out above 142, added 25% to position, stop moved to 138', 'Read TSM Q1 filing -- gross margins held above 53%, thesis intact', 'Watchlist: PLTR setup forming on the daily, level to watch is 24.50', and a note 'Powell speech moved 10Y yields +8bps, regime read still range-bound risk-on'. Yesterday's journal (2026-04-02) has open loops: '- [ ] Re-read MSFT 10-Q before earnings' and '- [ ] Set alert on SPY 510'.
tests.expected_output: A compiled journal entry with Positions / Trades, Theses & Research, Watchlist Activity, Market Recap, and Open Loops sections. Should ask clarifying questions before saving.
assertions: Does the output use the file path Daily/2026-04-03.md?
Does the YAML frontmatter include tags with [journal] and the date 2026-04-03?
Does it classify the NVDA add and stop move under Positions / Trades (not under another section)?
Does it classify the TSM filing read under Theses & Research?
Does it classify the PLTR setup under Watchlist Activity?
Does it carry forward at least one open loop from yesterday?
Does K2B-Investment ask at least one clarifying question about the captures before saving?
Are empty sections omitted (no Insights section if no insight was expressed)?
Does it treat the Powell macro note as Market Recap rather than a position trade?

tests.prompt: Run /journal for 2026-04-04 (evening). No new vault captures today. One session wrap-up exists in raw/research/: '2026-04-04_session-wrapup-journal-redesign.md' about redesigning the /journal skill. Yesterday's journal has open loop: '- [ ] Re-read MSFT 10-Q before earnings'. No other vault notes created today.
tests.expected_output: A minimal journal entry with just Theses & Research (the wrap-up reference) and Open Loops. Should note the quiet day and ask if anything was missed.
assertions: Does it reference the journal redesign session wrap-up somewhere appropriate (Theses & Research or a meta/notes line)?
Does it carry forward the MSFT 10-Q open loop?
Does it omit Positions / Trades section (no position activity)?
Does it omit Watchlist Activity section (no watchlist captures)?
Does K2B-Investment ask something like 'quiet trading day -- did anything actually move in the book?' or similar?
Does each section use bullet points, not paragraphs?

tests.prompt: Run /journal for 2026-04-05 (morning). No captures yet today. Yesterday's journal (2026-04-04) has open loops: '- [ ] Re-read MSFT 10-Q before earnings' and '- [ ] Decide on AAPL trim ahead of FOMC'.
tests.expected_output: A brief morning mode response showing yesterday's open loops and inviting Keith to capture throughout the trading day.
assertions: Does it show yesterday's open loops (MSFT 10-Q and AAPL trim)?
Does it link to yesterday's note as [[2026-04-04]]?
Does it NOT create a full compiled journal entry (morning mode should be brief)?
Does it suggest running /journal again at the close to compile?
Does the filename follow YYYY-MM-DD.md convention?

tests.prompt: Run /journal for 2026-04-06 (evening). Captures: 'NVDA Q4 earnings call -- beat top and bottom, guide raised, datacenter +73% YoY', 'Stock fading the print though, watching 138 hold', 'Quick thought -- the AI capex cycle commentary from hyperscalers all week is starting to look like a regime signal worth writing up'. No yesterday open loops. No prior session wrap-ups.
tests.expected_output: A journal entry with Theses & Research, Positions / Trades (or Watchlist Activity for the price-level watch), and possibly Insights. All from today's captures only.
assertions: Does it classify the NVDA earnings call recap under Theses & Research?
Does it classify the AI capex regime observation under Insights?
Does it handle the price-level watch (138 hold) under Positions / Trades or Watchlist Activity (not Insights)?
Are there no empty sections with blank bullets?
Does it ask at least one question (e.g., about the NVDA position size or any open loops)?
